What I Do

Four disciplines.
One sharp focus.

01
◻

Landing Page Copy

Your landing page has three seconds to earn the next scroll. I write pages that lead with the buyer's psychology, not the product's features — because attention is earned word by word.

  • Hero headline & subhead that stops the scroll
  • Conversion-mapped copy structure
  • CTA sequences that reduce drop-off
  • A/B variant copy for testing
02
◈

Marketing Copy

Ad copy, email sequences, social posts, product descriptions. Copy written from confirmed strategy — not from a template. Every line earns its place or it gets cut.

  • Facebook & Instagram ad copy
  • Email sequences (welcome, nurture, sales)
  • Ad teardowns — bad ad in, good ad out
  • Brand messaging frameworks
03
◎

Analytics & Reporting

Data that you can actually act on. I translate your campaign numbers into clear decisions — what's working, what's bleeding budget, and what to do next. No jargon. No ambiguity.

  • Campaign performance audits
  • Attribution analysis
  • Funnel conversion diagnostics
  • Monthly performance reports
04
△

Incrementality Testing

Most businesses can't tell you if their marketing is actually causing growth — or just correlating with it. Incrementality testing answers that question with data, not assumption.

  • Test design & holdout methodology
  • Geo-lift & matched market tests
  • Media mix contribution analysis
  • Budget reallocation recommendations
Why It Matters
"The Nairobi buyer doesn't think like a case study. They think like someone navigating real life."

Every framework I use is grounded in how Kenyan startups and SMEs actually operate — the market pressure, the budget realities, the pace of trust-building, the cultural codes that make copy land differently here than anywhere else.

Generic marketing advice is written for generic markets. What you get here is built from inside the city — from understanding what moves a buyer in Westlands, Kilimani, or Karen as much as one in Mombasa or Kisumu.

Let's Talk About Your Business
The Philosophy

Most marketing fails
before it starts.

The brief is wrong. The audience is assumed. The copy is written before the customer is understood. I've watched businesses pour budget into campaigns that were confidently pointed in the wrong direction — and the tragedy isn't the spend, it's that nobody questions whether the spend is working at all.

Incrementality is the discipline that changes that. It asks a simple question that most marketers avoid: would this sale have happened anyway? Not because growth is cynical, but because honest measurement is what separates marketing that compounds from marketing that just costs money.

Copy and analytics are not separate conversations. The best-performing landing page I've ever seen wasn't beautiful — it was precisely calibrated to the gap between what the buyer believed and what the product actually delivered. That gap is where every word I write lives.

How It Works

From WhatsApp
to live campaign.

01
Discovery Call
We talk through your business, your market, your current marketing, and what you actually need. No forms. Just a WhatsApp conversation.
02
Strategy Brief
I build a clear brief — audience, positioning, KPIs, and the specific angle your copy or analytics work will take. Agreed before anything is written.
03
Execution
Copy delivered in full. Analytics structured and readable. Test designs ready to run. Everything built around a single objective: measurable growth.
04
Review & Iterate
One round of revisions is built in. For ongoing work, we track, measure, and improve every cycle. Marketing should get sharper over time — not just more expensive.
What You Walk Away With

Concrete work.
Clear outcomes.

✓
Copy that converts
Landing pages, ads, and emails written to a specific audience with a specific objective. Not copy that sounds nice — copy that does a job.
✓
A readable analytics report
Numbers translated into decisions. What worked, what didn't, what to do with the next budget cycle. No 40-slide decks with nothing actionable inside.
✓
An incrementality test design
A rigorous methodology for isolating the true causal effect of your marketing — so you stop guessing which channels actually drive growth.
✓
Ad teardown + rewrite
Your existing ads, diagnosed and rewritten. What was wrong structurally, why the new version works, and a framework to apply to future creative.
✓
A market-native perspective
Every brief is filtered through Nairobi — the buyer behaviour, the platform habits, the cultural context that makes copy land differently here.
✓
No retainer required
Start with a single project. No minimum commitment. If the work is good, you'll come back. That's the only retention strategy I believe in.
